questions.text: Predict the decomposition product which will give brown colored gas
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Alkaline earth metal nitrates, such as magnesium nitrate (Mg(NO3)2), calcium nitrate (Ca(NO2)2), and barium nitrate (Ba(NO3)2), decompose upon heating to form metal oxides, nitrogen oxides, and oxygen gas. In each case, the metal nitrate decomposes into the corresponding metal oxide, nitrogen dioxide gas (NO2), and oxygen gas (O2). The nitrogen dioxide gas gives off the brown colour.

questions.text: Li2CO3 is thermally unstable whereas other Group-I carbonates are stable. Predict the reason.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: Li+ can effectively polarize CO3-2 ion
Li+ cannot effectively polarize CO3-2 ion

questions.explanation: The thermal instability of lithium carbonate (Li2CO3) compared to other alkali metal carbonates due to following reasons:Size and Charge of Lithium Ion (Li^+): Lithium ions are smaller and have a higher charge density compared to other alkali metal ions. Ionic Radius of Lithium: The small ionic radius of lithium ions.Carbonate Ion StabilityHigher Basicity of Lithium Oxide: The product of lithium carbonate decomposition, lithium oxide (Li2O), is a stronger base compared to lithium carbonate. Overall, the combination of these factors leads to the greater thermal instability of lithium carbonate compared to other alkali metal carbonates.

questions.text: Identify the nature of the ligand
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: EDTA is a hexadentate ligand, meaning it can form six bonds with a metal ion.

questions.text: Label the element in the first series of outer transition elements that has highest binding energy.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: When we move from left to right in the 3d series, the number of unpaired electrons increases upto V-B (vanadium) and VI-B (Chromium). After chromium, pairing of electron starts. Since chromium has the maximum number of unpaired electrons in its neutral state so it has the maximum binding energy.

questions.text: What is the IUPAC name of alkene which will give propanone on Ozonolysis?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: The ozonolysis of 2-methyl-2-butene (also known as tert-butyl ethylene) proceeds as follows:﻿The double bond in 2-methyl-2-butene reacts with ozone (O3) to form an ozonide intermediate.The ozonide intermediate then undergoes a reductive workup, typically using a reducing agent such as dimethyl sulfide (CH3)2S or zinc (Zn), to yield carbonyl compounds.The ozonolysis of 2-methyl-2-butene produces two different carbonyl compounds due to the branching of the molecule. One product is acetone (propanone), and the other is a ketone derived from the alkene's structure.

questions.text: Propose the types of Isomerism shown by 1-Butanol.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: Chain isomerism, Position isomerism & Functional group isomerism
Position isomerism, Functional group isomerism & Metamerism

questions.explanation: 1-Butanol (n-butanol or butyl alcohol) exhibits several types of isomerism:Chain Isomerism: As mentioned earlier, 1-butanol is a straight-chain alcohol. It consists of a linear chain of four carbon atoms. Other isomers with the same molecular formula (C4H10O) but different arrangements of the carbon skeleton, such as branched-chain isomers like 2-butanol and isobutanol, exhibit chain isomerism.Position Isomerism: 1-Butanol also shows position isomerism. In this case, the hydroxyl (-OH) group can be attached to any of the four carbon atoms in the carbon chain, resulting in different structural isomers. For example, 2-butanol and isobutanol are position isomers of 1-butanol because the hydroxyl group is attached to different carbon atoms in the chain.Functional Group Isomerism: Functional group isomerism involves compounds with the same molecular formula but different functional groups. For 1-butanol, this type of isomerism doesn't apply because it is an alcohol, and there are no other functional groups present in the molecule.

questions.text: What is the name of Grignard Reagent that will give 2,2-Dimethyl Propanoic Acid on reaction with CO2?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Neopentylmagnesium bromide, often referred to as neo-butyl magnesium bromide, is a Grignard reagent derived from neopentyl bromide. On recation with CO2, it will yield 2,2-dimethyl propanoic acid.

questions.text: Predict the product of acid hydrolysis of CH3 -C≡C- CH3 in the presence of HgSO4 catalyst.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: The acid hydrolysis of an alkyne, such as CH3-C≡C-CH3, in the presence of a mercury(II) sulfate (HgSO4) catalyst proceeds through a process known as hydration. In this reaction, water adds across the triple bond of the alkyne to form a ketone. The ketone formed is butanone.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: The iodoform test is a chemical test used to identify the presence of methyl ketones or secondary alcohols containing a methyl group adjacent to a carbonyl group (α-methyl ketones or secondary alcohols) in a given compound.R-CH2-OH+NaOH→ CHI3​(s) + R-COONa + 3H2O

questions.text: Identify the name of the compound which will give acetamide on heating?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Carboxylic acids react with ammonia to form ammonium salts which on heating produce acid amides. CH3COOH + NH3 → CH3COONH4+ → CH3CONH2 + H2O (HEAT)

questions.text: Predict which maltose is the disaccharide of
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Maltose is a disaccharide composed of two glucose molecules joined by a glycosidic bond. Specifically, maltose is formed by the condensation reaction between two glucose molecules, resulting in the elimination of a water molecule.

questions.text: Predict which one of the following substance is used as film forming agent in nail polish?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Among the substances commonly used in nail polish, one stands out as the primary film-forming agent:﻿Ethyl acetate: Ethyl acetate is another solvent commonly used in nail polish formulations. It helps dissolve other ingredients and aids in achieving the desired consistency of the nail polish. However, like acetone and toluene, it is not primarily used as a film-forming agent.

questions.text: Identify which one of the following water pollutant is Carcinogenic?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons (PAHs) are a group of organic compounds that are formed during incomplete combustion of organic matter, such as fossil fuels, wood, and tobacco. Many PAHs are known or suspected carcinogens, meaning they have the potential to cause cancer in humans. Explanation will be added soon.

questions.text: Mass spectrum of Magnesium shows that it contains three isotopes. Mg-24 (78.70%), Mg-25 (10.13%) & Mg-26(11.17%). The average atomic mass of Mg is
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: To calculate the average atomic mass, we use the formula:Average atomic mass=(Abundance1×Atomic mass1)+(Abundance2×Atomic mass2)+(Abundance3×Atomic mass3)Substituting the values:Average atomic mass=(0.7899×23.98504 )+(0.1000×24.98584 )+(0.1101×25.98259 )Average atomic mass=18.99254 +2.49858 +2.86561 Average atomic mass=24.35673 So, the average atomic mass of magnesium is approximately 24.35673

questions.text: Identify the functional group present in the Anhydride.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: In organic chemistry, organic acid anhydrides contain the functional group −C(=O)−O−C(=O)−. Organic acid anhydrides often form when one equivalent of water is removed from two equivalents of an organic acid in a dehydration reaction.

questions.text: Amines are basic in nature. Identify which one of the following is more basic?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: The basicity of an amine depends on its ability to donate an electron pair to form a bond with a proton (H+) in a Bronsted-Lowry acid-base reaction. Generally, the more available the lone pair of electrons on the nitrogen atom, the more basic the amine is. Given these factors, the general order of basicity for amines is:Tertiary amine > Secondary amine > Primary amineThis is because tertiary amines have three alkyl groups attached to the nitrogen, which provide electron density, making the lone pair more available for donation.

questions.text: Identify the most reactive Alcohol with respect to the breakage of O-H bond.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: The order of reactivity if alcohols with respect to O-H bond cleavage is:CH3OH> Primary alcohol> Secondary alcohol> Tertiary alcohol
